sample_inserted_mappings.promise_title: Reduce the federal crack-powder sentencing disparity
sample_inserted_mappings.outcome_summary: Federal law reduced the crack-to-powder cocaine sentencing disparity from 100-to-1 to 18-to-1.

sample_inserted_mappings.promise_title: Advance racial equity across federal agencies
sample_inserted_mappings.outcome_summary: The federal government created an equity review framework, but implementation remained uneven across agencies and over time.
No federal voting-rights restoration law was enacted despite high-profile legislative pushes.

sample_inserted_mappings.promise_title: Deliver stronger anti-redlining and Black homeownership gains
sample_inserted_mappings.outcome_summary: No durable anti-redlining or Black homeownership enforcement framework matching the public promise was delivered.
Federal policing reform remained active as a national policy priority, but Congress did not complete a durable statutory settlement.
The administration reduced the detainee population and repeatedly pursued closure, but Guantanamo remained open.
Combat brigades were withdrawn and the formal combat mission ended, but the broader conflict and later U.S. re-engagement undercut the full promise frame.
The policy was repealed and openly gay and lesbian Americans were allowed to serve in the military.
Federal law established broad insurance protections for people with pre-existing medical conditions.

sample_inserted_mappings.promise_title: Enact a credit card bill of rights
sample_inserted_mappings.outcome_summary: Federal law placed new limits on retroactive rate hikes and several abusive billing practices.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Reduce the federal crack-powder sentencing disparity
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Federal law reduced the crack-to-powder cocaine sentencing disparity from 100-to-1 to 18-to-1.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Advance racial equity across federal agencies
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The federal government created an equity review framework, but implementation remained uneven across agencies and over time.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Restore stronger federal voting-rights protections
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: No federal voting-rights restoration law was enacted despite high-profile legislative pushes.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Deliver stronger anti-redlining and Black homeownership gains
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: No durable anti-redlining or Black homeownership enforcement framework matching the public promise was delivered.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Increase federal policing accountability standards
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Federal policing reform remained active as a national policy priority, but Congress did not complete a durable statutory settlement.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Close Guantanamo Bay detention facility
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration reduced the detainee population and repeatedly pursued closure, but Guantanamo remained open.

inserted_rows.promise_title: End combat-brigade deployment in Iraq
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Combat brigades were withdrawn and the formal combat mission ended, but the broader conflict and later U.S. re-engagement undercut the full promise frame.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Repeal Don't Ask, Don't Tell
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The policy was repealed and openly gay and lesbian Americans were allowed to serve in the military.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Ban pre-existing-condition exclusions
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Federal law established broad insurance protections for people with pre-existing medical conditions.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Enact a credit card bill of rights
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Federal law placed new limits on retroactive rate hikes and several abusive billing practices.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Restore the ability to challenge pay discrimination
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Workers gained a renewed ability to file claims based on each discriminatory paycheck rather than only the original pay decision.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Enact a worker tax credit
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Workers received federal tax relief, but the policy was time-limited and later replaced by other tax-cut mechanisms.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Expand children's health insurance coverage
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Federal children's health coverage was expanded through reauthorization and program growth.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Ease federal restrictions on embryonic stem cell research
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Federal policy was changed to allow broader support for embryonic stem cell research subject to legal and ethical rules.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Roll back Bush tax cuts for upper-income households
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Top-end Bush tax cuts were partially rolled back, but the final result came as a negotiated compromise.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Withdraw the United States from TPP
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The United States formally withdrew from TPP negotiations.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Build a southern border wall and make Mexico pay
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Barrier construction advanced in some places, but the core promise that Mexico would pay and that a comprehensive wall would be built was not met.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Exit the Paris climate agreement
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The United States exited the Paris Agreement before later rejoining under Biden.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Move the U.S. embassy in Israel to Jerusalem
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The U.S. embassy was moved to Jerusalem, delivering the central promise.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Replace NAFTA with a new trade deal
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: NAFTA was replaced by USMCA.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Appoint conservative Supreme Court justices
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Trump delivered on the promise to appoint conservative Supreme Court justices.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Repeal and replace the Affordable Care Act
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The Affordable Care Act was not repealed, though some regulatory and tax changes weakened parts of the law.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Impose a travel ban on targeted countries
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: A narrower set of country-based travel bans took effect after legal challenges, but the original campaign wording was not implemented as stated.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Deliver a major federal tax cut
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Trump delivered a large tax cut package centered on corporate-rate reduction and broad tax changes.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Pass federal criminal justice reform
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Federal sentencing and prison-policy reforms were enacted through the First Step Act.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Rejoin the Paris climate accord
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The United States rejoined the Paris Agreement early in Biden's term.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Revoke the Keystone XL pipeline permit
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The Keystone XL project was stopped after the permit revocation.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Nominate the first Black woman to the Supreme Court
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Jackson was confirmed and became the first Black woman to serve on the Supreme Court.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Forgive public-college tuition debt for many borrowers
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Biden delivered substantial targeted debt relief, but the original broad promise was only partially achieved after the Supreme Court blocked the administration's main plan.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Advance racial equity across the federal government
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration established a federal equity framework, but implementation remained uneven and administratively vulnerable.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Pass a bipartisan infrastructure package
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration delivered a major infrastructure law with long-term federal spending commitments.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Reverse the ban on transgender military service
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Transgender Americans regained the ability to serve openly in the military.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Deliver 100 million COVID-19 vaccine shots in the first 100 days
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration met and then exceeded the original 100 million shot target within the first 100 days.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Raise the federal minimum wage to $15
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: No federal $15 minimum wage was enacted during Biden's term.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Strengthen federal labor protections
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Federal labor policy moved in a more worker-friendly direction administratively, but major legislative reform did not become law.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Launch a homeowner stabilization and foreclosure-prevention effort
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration created a much larger foreclosure-relief framework than promised, but the homeowner-stabilization effort underperformed and failed to prevent broad foreclosure damage.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Ban deceptive election practices and voter intimidation
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The specific federal legislation to penalize deceptive election practices and require corrective federal action never became law.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Ensure long-term federal funding for HBCUs
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Trump delivered notable federal support to HBCUs through executive attention and the FUTURE Actâ€™s long-term funding framework.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Increase access to affordable housing
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Biden increased housing assistance and launched a federal housing-supply agenda, but the broader affordable-housing platform was only partially realized.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Make HBCUs and under-resourced MSIs more affordable
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Biden delivered major federal support to HBCUs and other minority-serving institutions and paired that with student-relief steps, but he did not enact the full tuition-grant vision described in the campaign promise.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Restore voting rights after felony sentences are completed
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration supported broader voting-rights legislation, but it did not create the promised incentives for states to automatically restore voting rights after felony sentences are completed.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Make it easier for workers to unionize
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The labor-law reform promised through the Employee Free Choice Act did not become federal law.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Make no cuts to Medicaid
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Trump did not keep the promise to avoid Medicaid cuts, as repeal efforts and budget proposals repeatedly put the program at risk.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Offer a public option health insurance plan
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: No national public-option health plan was enacted during Biden's presidency.

inserted_rows.promise_title: End the federal use of private prisons and detention centers
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Biden partially reduced the federal use of private prisons, but the broader federal detention system did not fully exit private detention contracts.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Eliminate cash bail
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: No national federal policy eliminating cash bail was enacted during Biden's presidency.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Direct 40 percent of key clean-energy benefits to disadvantaged communities
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Biden created the Justice40 framework and linked it to major federal investment programs, but implementation quality and measurable benefit delivery remained uneven.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Suppress Ku Klux Klan violence and protect Black voting rights
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Grant used expanded federal enforcement powers against Ku Klux Klan violence, producing a concrete period of federal protection for Black voting rights during Reconstruction.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Pass the Voting Rights Act after Selma
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Johnson delivered the central promise by helping secure and sign the Voting Rights Act of 1965.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Pass federal fair-housing protections
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Johnson delivered federal fair-housing protections through enactment of the Fair Housing Act in 1968.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Ban racial profiling in federal law enforcement
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The Obama administration narrowed some federal profiling practices, but it did not deliver a complete or fully durable federal ban matching the campaign promise.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Eliminate the federal death penalty
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Biden paused federal executions and later commuted most federal death-row sentences, but he did not abolish the federal death penalty.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Appoint Thurgood Marshall to the Supreme Court
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Johnson delivered the appointment by nominating and securing confirmation of Thurgood Marshall to the Supreme Court.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Expand affirmative-action requirements in federal contracting
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Nixon administration policy expanded affirmative-action enforcement in federal contracting through the Philadelphia Plan framework.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Expand the Child Tax Credit and make it fully refundable
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Biden delivered a large temporary Child Tax Credit expansion, but he did not make the fuller refundable expansion permanent.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Establish a federal minimum wage and maximum-hours standard
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Roosevelt delivered national wage and hour standards, but the original framework excluded many occupations in which Black workers were heavily concentrated.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Create old-age insurance and unemployment protections through Social Security
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Roosevelt delivered a foundational federal social-insurance system, but the original law excluded many workers in sectors where Black Americans were disproportionately employed.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Sign the Civil Rights Act of 1875
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Grant signed a federal public-accommodations and equal-treatment law, representing a major Reconstruction-era civil-rights protection, though later Supreme Court decisions limited its long-term enforcement.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Sign the Civil Rights Act of 1964
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Johnson delivered the central anti-discrimination law of the Civil Rights era through enactment of the Civil Rights Act of 1964.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Issue Executive Order 11246 on nondiscrimination and affirmative action in federal contracting
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Johnson created a stronger executive framework for nondiscrimination and affirmative-action enforcement in federal contracting before later Nixon-era expansion.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Enforce school desegregation at Little Rock
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Eisenhower used federal executive power to enforce court-ordered school desegregation against state resistance in Little Rock.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Sign the Civil Rights Act of 1957
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Eisenhower signed the first modern federal civil-rights law since Reconstruction, rebuilding federal civil-rights enforcement capacity even in a limited form.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Sign the Voting Rights Act Reauthorization of 2006
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Bush preserved major Voting Rights Act protections by signing the 2006 reauthorization, extending federal voting-rights enforcement before later judicial rollback.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Desegregate the armed forces
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Truman formally committed the federal government to ending segregation in the armed services and began a consequential process of military desegregation.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Issue Executive Order 10925 on equal employment opportunity
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Kennedy created an early modern equal-employment enforcement framework in federal contracting, requiring proactive action against discrimination.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Issue Executive Order 11063 on nondiscrimination in federally assisted housing
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Kennedy created a federal nondiscrimination framework for federally assisted housing before the Fair Housing Act of 1968.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Sign the Community Reinvestment Act
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Carter signed a major anti-redlining law that created a durable federal framework for pushing banks to serve neglected communities more fairly.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Withdraw federal troops and accept Southern home rule
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Federal military and political protection for Reconstruction governments was withdrawn, and no comparable enforcement framework replaced it.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Allow federal enforcement to weaken after United States v. Cruikshank
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: Federal enforcement capacity against racial terror and rights violations weakened substantially late in Grant's presidency.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Retreat from federal civil-rights enforcement after the Civil Rights Cases
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The federal government accepted a narrower civil-rights enforcement role after the Civil Rights Cases without mounting a major replacement response.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Protect Black voting rights through the Lodge Elections Bill
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: No renewed federal election-protection law was enacted, and the strongest late-nineteenth-century restoration effort failed.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Accept "separate but equal" as the governing federal posture toward segregation
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The federal constitutional order gave durable legal protection to state segregation under the "separate but equal" framework.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Allow discriminatory voting restrictions to stand under federal constitutional review
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The federal constitutional order allowed states to preserve Black disfranchisement through facially race-neutral voting restrictions.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Myers v. Anderson invalidates Maryland's grandfather-clause voting law
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The Supreme Court invalidated Maryland's grandfather-clause voting law and affirmed that damages actions could proceed against election officials who enforced it.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Buchanan v. Warley invalidates Louisville's residential segregation ordinance
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The Supreme Court invalidated Louisville's race-based residential segregation ordinance.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Supreme Court denies equitable relief against Alabama voter disfranchisement claims in Giles v. Harris
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The Court did not grant the requested registration remedy and left the challenged voter-registration system in effect.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Supreme Court strikes down Oklahoma's grandfather clause in Guinn v. United States
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The decision invalidated Oklahoma's grandfather-clause suffrage amendment, while leaving open the continued use of literacy tests apart from that clause.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Supreme Court lets enforcement of a racially restrictive housing covenant stand in Corrigan v. Buckley
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The Supreme Court left judicial enforcement of the challenged racially restrictive covenant in effect.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Supreme Court bars judicial enforcement of racially restrictive covenants in Shelley v. Kraemer
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The Supreme Court barred state courts from enforcing racially restrictive covenants in housing transactions.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Supreme Court holds that 42 U.S.C. 1982 bars private racial discrimination in property transactions in Jones v. Alfred H. Mayer Co.
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The Supreme Court recognized a federal cause of action against private racial discrimination in the sale and rental of property under 42 U.S.C. 1982.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Congress passes and President signs the Fair Housing Act of 1968
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The Fair Housing Act created federal fair-housing protections covering discrimination based on race, color, religion, and national origin.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Congress passes and President signs the Fair Housing Amendments Act of 1988
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The Fair Housing Amendments Act strengthened fair-housing enforcement by expanding administrative and civil remedies and revising procedures under federal housing-discrimination law.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Supreme Court holds that disparate-impact claims are cognizable under the Fair Housing Act in Texas Department of Housing and Community Affairs v. Inclusive Communities Project, Inc.
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The Supreme Court preserved disparate-impact liability under the Fair Housing Act while requiring plaintiffs to identify a specific policy and satisfy a robust causality standard.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Paying Our Great Transportation Security Administration Officers and Employees
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The memorandum created an official administration action on TSA compensation, but its broader measurable labor and equity effects remain incomplete without later implementation evidence.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Require proof of citizenship and paper-ballot election safeguards
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration created a formal federal election-integrity directive, but nationwide implementation was not complete.

inserted_rows.promise_title: End federal DEI and equity-based government programs
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration initiated the removal of federal DEI and equity structures across agencies.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Increase federal support for state and local law enforcement
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration formally expanded federal support for local law enforcement agencies through a 2025 executive order.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Clear street encampments and expand treatment-oriented street-order responses
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration formally prioritized anti-encampment enforcement and treatment-linked street-order policies.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Expand domestic production of critical medicines
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration initiated federal regulatory changes intended to support domestic pharmaceutical manufacturing.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Expand educational freedom and school-choice options for families
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration established a federal policy framework to support education-freedom initiatives.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Reform higher-education accreditation around student outcomes
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration initiated an accreditation-reform process centered on student outcomes and federal oversight.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Promote excellence and innovation at HBCUs
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration formally created a White House HBCU initiative and related advisory structure.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Reinstate school discipline policies without equity-based disparate-impact framing
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration began replacing prior federal school-discipline guidance with a different disciplinary framework.

inserted_rows.promise_title: Prepare Americans for high-paying skilled trade jobs
inserted_rows.outcome_summary: The administration began a federal workforce-policy effort centered on apprenticeships and skilled trades.
